english: tive'; lc̀um-dkár (lcum dkar) prob. another species of that plant Med.

1. resp. ljags (ljags) (जिह्वा) tongue, lc̀e rkyaṅ-ba (lce rkyang ba) to put forth, to show the tongue Mil.; lc̀e brgyá-yis yon-tan c̀uṅ-zad brjód-par nus ma mc̀is (lce brgya yis yon tan cung zad brjod par nus ma mchis) even with a hundred tongues we should not be able sufficiently to praise the merit... Pth. -- 2. blade, Cs. gri-lc̀e (gri lce). -- 3. (अशनि) thunderbolt, lc̀e ʿbébs-paí glog (lce 'bebs pa'i glog) a flash of lightning accompanying a thunderbolt. -- 4. flame, mé-lc̀e (me lce). Comp. lc̀e-kyigs (lce kyigs) the frenum of the tongue Cs. -- lc̀e-c̀uṅ (lce chung) uvula, lc̀e-c̀uṅ ʿbabs (lce chung 'babs) inflammation of the uvula Med. -- lc̀e-ynyís-pa (lce gnyis pa) double-tongued, deceitful, lc̀e-ynyís byéd-pa (lce gnyis byed pa) to be double-tongued. -- lc̀e-t́éb (lce theb), lc̀e-drá (lce dra) a fleshy excrescence below the tongue Cs. -- lc̀e-bdé (lce bde) a nimble tongue a babbler Mil. -- lc̀e-spyáṅ (lce spyang) = c̀e-spyáṅ (ce spyang) Thgy., Stg. -- lc̀e-ʿbúr (lce 'bur) a swelling on the tongue Cs. -- lc̀e-myaṅ-tsá (lce myang tsa) alum Med. -- lc̀e-rtsá (lce rtsa) the root of the tongue, lc̀e-rtsá-c̀an (lce rtsa can) a letter pronounced from the root of the tongue, a guttural. -- lc̀e-rtsé (lce rtse) the tip of the tongue Cs., lc̀e-rtsé-c̀an (lce rtse can) a letter sounded with the tip of the tongue, a lingual. -- lc̀e-tsá- (-ba) (lce tsa (ba)) a sharp-tasted, pungent medicinal herb Med. -- lc̀e-gźór (lce gzhor) a tongue-scraper Cs.
a coat of mail for a horse Sch.
to go to kill one's self, to seek death, esp. by a leap into the water or down a precipice, but not every kind of suicide; also used of insects that fly into a flame etc.
also lc̀óg-ma (lcog ma) or mo (mo) lark.
1. B., C. a turret on a house-top, pinnacle (W. *speu*). -- 2. v. lc̀óg-tse (lcog tse).
prob. low, lc̀óg-por skye (lcog por skye) (a certain plant) is low-growing, it does not grow high.

resp. gsol-lc̀óg (gsol lcog), table, in Tibet, esp. in W., a very rare piece of furniture, and always small and low; lc̀óg-kèbs (lcog kebs) table-cloth, lc̀óg-kèbs btiṅ-ba (lcog kebs bting ba) to lay the cloth; rgya-lc̀óg (rgya lcog) a large table, a European table; mdun-lc̀óg (mdun lcog) 'fore-table', a sort of table before an idol, for spreading offerings on it, v. e.g. Hook. I, 172; but it is not the same as altar.

I. to be agitated, to shake, to tremble, mé-tog mgo-lc̀óg (me tog mgo lcog) Zam. a flower shaking, waving its head (little used). II 1. vb. to be able, de ma lc̀óg-na (de ma lcog na) if (he) is not able (to do that); ji lc̀óg-kyi (ji lcog kyi) Mil. as much as possible, to the utmost; *ṅa-ráṅ-ghi gaṅ c̀óg-pa* (nga rang ghi gang cog pa) C. as far as I am able. More used: 2. adj. able, śèd-kyis mi lc̀óg-pa (shed kyis mi lcog pa) unable, feeble, weak, ríg-pas mi lc̀óg-pa (rig pas mi lcog pa) ignorant; *ṅe tsar c̀ig-la c̀óg-pa me'* (nge tsar cig la cog pa me') I am not able to carry the whole at once C.; *c̀óg-c̀an* (cog can) clever, skilful, handy, *c̀óg-méd* (cog med) awkward W.; *khē c̀og mi dug* (khe cog mi dug) he does not get on with his mouth, he lisps; also *kha c̀óg-pa* (kha cog pa) irreverent, disrespectful in speaking W.(?)
sbol-lc̀óṅ (sbol lcong) a frog in its first stage of development, tadpole Pth.
1. the letter č (ch), the aspirated c̀ (c), pronounced hard and forcibly, like ch in chap or church. -- 2. numerical figure: 6, ča-pa (cha pa) the sixth volume.

I. part. portion, share 1. opp. to the whole, ča gsúm-du bgos (cha gsum du bgos) divide it in three parts! brgyai ča (brgyai cha) 1/100 Glr.; stóṅ-gi ča (stong gi cha) 1/1000; baṅ-mdzód gsúm-ča gcig (bang mdzod gsum cha gcig) one third
